prose:
Fukui reactivity indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for benzaldehyde were computed using DFT with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier standard) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The Mulliken charge scheme was used to derive Fukui functions from neutral, cationic (+1, multiplicity 2), and anionic (−1, multiplicity 2) states. SCF converged in all cases; charge conservation checks passed (sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000). The most electrophilic atom is the aromatic carbon at position 3 (f⁺ = 0.3994), and the most nucleophilic atom is the aldehyde oxygen at position 1 (f⁻ = 0.2384). All Fukui function values per atom are tabulated above. Integrity check status: trustworthy = true. Visualization plot saved to the output directory.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
